title: Iran's speaker says US negotiations have progressed but far from final deal
subhead: Iran said it had been 'victorious in the field' during weeks of war and had only agreed to a temporary truce with the US because its demands had been met, Iranian Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said on Saturday. The two-week ceasefire is set to end on Wednesday unless it is renewed.

title: Obsession with primaries takes hold in French politics a year out from presidential race

In southern Lebanon, displaced people return to their bombed villages
subhead: As the ceasefire takes effect, residents of southern Lebanon, Hezbollah's stronghold, return to their villages to witness the destruction caused by Israeli airstrikes.

title: Louis Verret paints the drama of football in watercolor
subhead: The artist based in Aubervilliers, a suburb of Paris, paints football's masters as if they are freeze-frames, channeling pure emotion straight to the goal.

title: Why it's important to teach children to recognize birdsong
subhead: Knowing which bird is chirping or warbling is actually child's play, as long as you approach it methodically. We asked experts to guide us in this learning process. Along the way, they gave some compelling reasons to distinguish the blackbird's song from the chaffinch's tune.

title: A photographic journey into the daily lives of home care aides
subhead: Between autumn 2024 and summer 2025, photographer Vincent Gouriou followed several home health aides in Dordogne, southwest France, documenting their often difficult daily routines as they traveled to isolated homes, provided care and offered a listening ear.

Ireland, striving to become Europe's data center hub, falls victim to its own success
subhead: Despite major pressure on the power grid and the risk of rising greenhouse gas emissions, Dublin wants to keep building these facilities for tech giants and establish itself as a European leader in the sector.

title: 'Dansons,' Céline Dion's new single, already feels outdated
subhead: The superstar's new song, written by Jean-Jacques Goldman and launched with great fanfare on Friday, seems to belong to another era. It is far from the hits that the French singer-songwriter created for the Québécois diva in the 1990s.

title: Saint-Denis Mayor Bally Bagayoko: 'When you win Paris's largest suburb, it gives you a different standing'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Bally Bagayoko says he can play a role in uniting the left around La France Insoumise leader Jean-Luc Mélenchon for the 2027 French presidential election.
